title.th: ตรวจและรักษา ก่อนจะสายเกินไป

hook.en: Only 1 in 5 Thais at high risk of NCDs receives preventive intervention before the disease develops.
hook.th: มีคนไทยเพียง 1 ใน 5 ที่มีความเสี่ยงสูงต่อโรค NCDs ที่ได้รับการแทรกแซงเชิงป้องกันก่อนที่โรคจะพัฒนาขึ้น

challenge.en: How might we build a predictive health risk platform that identifies high-risk individuals early and triggers personalized preventive action — before symptoms appear?
challenge.th: เราจะสร้างแพลตฟอร์มประเมินความเสี่ยงด้านสุขภาพเชิงพยากรณ์ที่ระบุตัวบุคคลที่มีความเสี่ยงสูงได้แต่เนิ่น ๆ และกระตุ้นให้เกิดการป้องกันแบบเฉพาะบุคคลก่อนที่อาการจะปรากฏได้อย่างไร?

severity.justification: 74-77% of Thai deaths are from NCDs (400,000 annually). 27% of diabetics and 48% of hypertensives are unaware of their condition.

difficulty.justification: Requires healthcare system transformation from treatment-focused to prevention-focused, fragmented data systems, behavioral intervention gaps.

impact.justification: THB 211B investment could save 310,000 lives and return THB 430B. Early intervention prevents complications and reduces healthcare costs.

urgency.justification: Thailand is rapidly aging; each year of delayed action means more undiagnosed cases progressing to complications.

statistics.stat: NCDs account for 74-77% of all deaths in Thailand (400,000 annually)

sources.label: Ministry of Public Health Thailand / WHO

statistics.stat: 27% of diabetics and 48% of hypertensives in Thailand are unaware of their condition
THB 1.6 trillion annual economic cost from NCDs (9.7% of GDP)
Only 4.9% of eligible Thais used annual health checkups in 2015
THB 211B investment could save 310,000 lives and return THB 430B

sources.label: Health Intervention and Technology Assessment Program (HITAP)

affectedPopulations.size: Estimated 3-5 million Thais with undiagnosed pre-diabetes or pre-hypertension

rootCauses.cause: Healthcare system designed for treatment, not prevention
rootCauses.explanation: Current system incentivizes treatment over prevention; NCD clinics focus on diagnosed patients; no systematic risk stratification.
Patient data scattered across facilities; no unified risk scoring; limited predictive analytics capability.
Lack of AI/ML tools for risk prediction; no integration of lifestyle, genetic, and clinical data for personalized risk assessment.
Limited personalized behavior change programs; no continuous engagement with at-risk individuals; one-size-fits-all approaches.
Health, education, agriculture, urban planning operate in silos; no integrated approach to NCD prevention.

existingSolutions.name: NCD Plus Clinics
existingSolutions.approach: Ministry of Public Health facilities providing comprehensive NCD care at district hospital level

existingSolutions.name: Village Health Volunteers Program
existingSolutions.approach: 1 million community volunteers providing basic health services and health promotion

existingSolutions.name: 30 Baht Scheme Expansion
existingSolutions.approach: Universal coverage expansion to include prevention services at any facility

opportunityAreas.description: Build ML models integrating clinical, lifestyle, and environmental data to predict NCD risk and trigger personalized interventions
opportunityAreas.potentialImpact: Identify high-risk individuals before disease develops; enable targeted prevention

opportunityAreas.description: Equip VHVs with mobile tools for risk screening, health education, and follow-up tracking
opportunityAreas.potentialImpact: Scale prevention to community level; continuous engagement with at-risk individuals

opportunityAreas.description: Digital platform delivering personalized lifestyle interventions based on individual risk profiles
opportunityAreas.potentialImpact: Sustained behavior change; reduced disease progression

resources.title: Thailand National Health Examination Survey
resources.description: Comprehensive national survey providing NCD prevalence and risk factor data

resources.title: HITAP NCD Prevention Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
resources.description: Economic analysis of NCD prevention interventions in Thailand

resources.title: WHO Package of Essential NCD Interventions (WHO PEN)
resources.description: WHO framework for NCD prevention and management at primary care level

keyInsights: The economic case is compelling: THB 211B investment could save 310,000 lives and return THB 430B.
91% of NCD costs come from lost productivity, not treatment - prevention is economically essential.
Thailand has key assets: 1M+ VHVs, 9,800+ PHC centers, universal coverage, growing digital health adoption.
The gap is not infrastructure but system design: healthcare is organized for treatment, not prevention.

tangibleEquivalent.en: Like ignoring a leaky roof until the house floods. 400,000 lives lost yearly.
tangibleEquivalent.th: เหมือนเพิกเฉยต่อหลังคารั่วจนน้ำท่วมบ้าน 400,000 ชีวิตสูญเสียทุกปี
